# Amazon Q Developer User Guide - Complete Document History

## Overview
This is the document history for the Amazon Q Developer User Guide, tracking all updates from November 28, 2023 (initial preview release) through February 18, 2026.

## Key Updates by Category

### Recent Updates (2026)

| Date | Change |
|------|--------|
| **February 18, 2026** | Updated account limit for subscriptions: Maximum increased from 10 to 20 accounts per AWS Region, per organization |
| **January 16, 2026** | Deprecated code transformation for GitLab - `/q transform` quick action and CI/CD pipeline customization removed |
| **January 13, 2026** | Removed deprecated GitLab quick actions (`/q dev (revise)` and `/q test` unit test generation) |

### 2025 Updates (Comprehensive)

#### December 2025
- **December 29**: Updated GitHub feature development workflow to use conversation-based feedback with `/q` command instead of traditional GitHub review process
- **December 18**: Updated GitHub app installation registration steps for Kiro and Amazon Q Developer shared console
- **December 15**: Deprecated code transformation for Java modernization in GitHub

#### November 2025
- **November 17**: Q CLI renamed to Kiro CLI

#### October 2025
- **October 29**: Updated managed policies (AmazonQFullAccess, AmazonQDeveloperAccess, AWSServiceRoleForUserSubscriptions)
- **October 21**:
  - Removed IDE agent documentation - replaced `/dev`, `/doc/`, `/test`, `/review` agents with agentic chat capabilities
  - Added IDE memory bank documentation for generating memory banks for Amazon Q chat
  - Added Q CLI feature documentation:
    - Creating agents with AI assistance
    - Prompt management
    - Responding to messages
    - Project rules

#### September 2025
- **September 23**: Enhanced context management documentation with comparison tables, decision flowcharts, and best practices
- **September 18**: Added Q CLI v1.16.0 feature documentation:
  - Agent default behavior with priority-based selection
  - Legacy MCP configuration support
  - New chat commands: `/tangent tail`, `/changelog`
  - Agent-related settings
- **September 17**:
  - Context hooks deprecated in favor of agent hooks
  - Added remote MCP server support and built-in tools documentation
- **September 9**:
  - Removed Amazon S3 buckets from allowlist table
  - New command line tool for transformation version with bug fixes
- **September 2**:
  - Updated GitHub review agent content with code review summary and threaded findings
  - Added prerequisites: Write, Maintain, or Admin role required in GitHub for code reviews

#### August 2025
- **August 21**: MCP can be disabled for organizations
- **August 19**: Updated proxy topic - Proxy Auto-Configuration (PAC) files not supported
- **August 15**:
  - Updated deployment options: maximum 10 accounts per AWS Region per organization
  - Added comprehensive troubleshooting documentation
  - Added troubleshooting logs section and log access/analysis guide
- **August 7**: Added `qct history` command documentation for viewing transformation job history
- **August 6**:
  - Added transformation job history feature for Visual Studio Code
  - Updated subscription visibility topic
- **August 5**: GitLab self-managed instances require GitLab 17.11.0 or later
- **August 1**: Added procedures for running code reviews with agentic chat in Visual Studio Code

#### July 2025
- **July 31**:
  - Added model selection for IDE chat
  - Added keyboard shortcuts topic for all IDEs and platforms
  - Added custom agents feature for CLI customization
- **July 30**: Added chat history compaction topic for managing context window limits
- **July 24**: Added profile deletion topic
- **July 21**: Added opt-out steps for telemetry collection during command line transformation
- **July 16**: Updated GitHub workflow with new page for handling first-party dependencies
- **July 15**:
  - Updated user activity report metrics for inline chat feature
  - Updated proxy configuration for Eclipse, JetBrains, Visual Studio
- **July 9**: Image context support added to IDE chat - users can now include images for UI mockups and sequence diagrams
- **July 7**: Updated Builder ID upgrade section - cannot link multiple Builder IDs to single AWS account
- **July 2**: Updated profile sharing section - clarified that management account settings don't override member accounts

#### June 2025
- **June 30**: Added slash commands details for GitHub (feature development, code reviews, code transformation, help)
- **June 26**:
  - New content for creating project rules in GitLab and GitHub
  - Details about QCT CLI in IDEs (security, pausing, canceling, new parameters, LLM summary output)
  - QCT CLI now supports `https://q.eu-central-1.amazonaws.com/`
- **June 25**:
  - Updated KMS policy with permissions: `kms:Decrypt`, `kms:DescribeKey`, `kms:Encrypt`, `kms:GenerateDataKey`
  - Added proxy configuration for Visual Studio Code
- **June 24**:
  - User activity reports generated at midnight UTC, split into files with 1,000 users each
  - Updated S3 bucket section - Visual Studio Code plugin v1.72.0+ doesn't require bucket allowlisting
  - Rebranded "Amazon Q Developer operational investigations" to "CloudWatch investigations"
- **June 19**: GitHub slash commands for feature development and code transformation in issue comments
- **June 18**:
  - Rules button added to VS Code IDE chat for creating/managing project rules
  - Pinned context support in VS Code for maintaining context across interactions
- **June 17**:
  - Updated code transformation details (source/target versions, upgrade requirements)
  - Added "Chat with Amazon Q about network security" feature
  - Added Data storage topic explaining where content is stored
- **June 12**: MCP servers can now be used with Amazon Q Developer in the IDE
- **June 11**: Personal accounts (Builder IDs) can now upgrade to Pro tier
- **June 9**:
  - Updated subscription statuses ("Unavailable" status clarified)
  - Support for specifying dependencies for CLI transformations with dependency upgrade file
  - New command reference section for Q CLI arguments
  - Model selection for command line chat
- **June 5**:
  - Chat context support expanded to JetBrains, Visual Studio, Eclipse IDEs
  - Agentic chat available in all supported IDEs
- **June 2**:
  - GitHub code reviews now performed in pull requests with `/q review` command
  - Cost optimization capabilities added (in addition to cost analysis)
- **May 22**: Updated firewall page with new URLs
- **May 21**: MCP configuration commands available from command line
- **May 16**: Updated customization policy topic with permission error information
- **May 15**:
  - Troubleshooting Amazon Q Developer Pro subscriptions updated
  - Automatic code reviews triggered in new/reopened GitLab merge requests and GitHub pull requests
  - Updated VPC endpoints
  - Improved MCP server loading (background initialization)
  - Added image support in chat with `fs_read` tool
  - Added conversation persistence with `/export` and `/import` commands
  - Updated managed policies (AmazonQFullAccess, AmazonQDeveloperAccess) for conversation history management
- **May 14**: Support for multiple conversations in console chat with save/switch functionality
- **May 13**: Updated subscription details topic
- **May 12**: Transformation website removed
- **May 10**: Terminology updated - "identity-aware console sessions" to "identity-enhanced console sessions"
- **May 8**: Added example SCP denying access outside EU regions
- **May 5**: Amazon Q Developer for GitHub information added
- **May 3**: Added context hooks support
- **May 2**: Managed policy AmazonQFullAccess updated
- **May 1**: Agentic chat functionality available in IDE
- **April 30**:
  - Updates to customizations - additional language support
  - Managed and example policies updated (permissions added to AmazonQFullAccess, GitLabDuoWithAmazonQPermissionsPolicy, admin plugin configuration)
  - Added MCP support in CLI
- **April 29**: Added upgrade details (Free tier to Pro tier)
- **April 28**: Conversation history now saved in IDE chat
- **April 21**:
  - Support for code as context (classes, functions, global variables)
  - Updated GitLab Duo with Amazon Q onboarding and policy
- **April 16**: Updated dashboard permissions for viewing Amazon Q Developer dashboard
- **April 15**: Updated list of required permissions for dashboard
- **April 13**:
  - Improved security documentation for command line
  - Enhanced tool permissions documentation with security best practices
- **April 12**: Updated subscription experience - workflow moved to Amazon Q Developer console
- **April 10**:
  - Inline chat available in Eclipse
  - Amazon Q Developer profiles available in Europe (Frankfurt) Region
  - `/tools` command added to CLI for managing tool permissions
- **April 9**: Support for natural languages other than English (IDE and CLI)
- **April 8**:
  - Updated GitLab Duo with Amazon Q (inline policy changes, optional CMK policy, `/fix` feature removed)
  - Email notifications for transformations
- **April 4**: Added topics for context, prompts, and project rules
- **April 4**: Corrected subscription topics
- **March 25**: Updated example policies with `sso:CreateInstance` permission
- **March 20**: Support for C++ and C# in customizations
- **March 13**: Updated chatting about resources - support for multiple AWS resources
- **March 12**: Documentation generation now supports C++ and C#
- **March 6**:
  - New subscription-related limit: maximum 50 AWS accounts in organization
  - Context integration added to CLI chat
- **February 28**:
  - New version of command line transformation tool with IAM authentication support
  - Policy correction (JSON syntax error fixed)
- **February 25**:
  - Added customizations policy permission
  - New dashboard topic with metrics descriptions
- **February 21**:
  - Added cross-region processing topic
  - Updated AWSServiceRoleForUserSubscriptions with additional permissions
- **February 20**:
  - `/doc` enhancement - generate infrastructure diagrams
  - Added new subscription-related topics (statuses and visibility)
- **February 19**:
  - Amazon Q Developer in chat applications - new chapter
  - Support for Java 21 transformations
- **February 14**:
  - New firewall topic
  - Command line transformation tool now supports embedded SQL conversion
- **February 12**: User activity report path corrected
- **February 10**: Transformed code retention increased from 24 hours to 30 days
- **February 7**: New subscription workflow
- **February 6**: Command line transformation tool updated with multi-commit support
- **February 3**: `/dev` enhancement - can now test generated code

#### January 2025
- **January 31**: Customizations section updated - include any number of repositories
- **January 24**: Added example prompt logs
- **January 23**: CloudZero plugin available in Amazon Q chat
- **January 15**: New user activity report metrics added

#### December 2024
- **December 16**:
  - Old dashboard information removed
  - New dashboard filters and metrics added
- **December 13**: Troubleshooting with Amazon Q section added
- **December 6**: Identity-enhanced sessions update with clarified instructions
- **December 3**:
  - New test generation agent for unit tests
  - Large-scale transformation for .NET, mainframe, VMware workloads
  - GitLab Duo with Amazon Q information added
  - Documentation generation (READMEs) in IDE
  - Code reviews in IDE (previously security scans)
  - .NET transformation in Visual Studio (preview)
  - Transformation on command line (preview)

#### November 2024
- **November 27**:
  - Multiple diffs for transformation in IDE
  - Amazon Q in Eclipse (preview)
- **November 26**: Cost analysis now generally available (was preview)
- **November 22**:
  - Transformation for embedded SQL code
  - Dashboard updated with new metrics
  - CodeConnections repositories - choose specific repositories for customization
- **November 21**: Amazon Q command line now supports Linux (Ubuntu 22 & 24, GNOME v42+, Xorg, IBus)
- **November 20**:
  - Customization in chat now generally available
  - Customizations support `.md`, `.mdx`, `.rst`, `.txt` files
  - Added supported IAM Identity Center Regions section
- **November 18**:
  - Language support expanded: Dart, Lua, R, Swift, SystemVerilog, PowerShell, JSON, YAML
  - Customer managed key support information added
  - Cross-region inference topic added
  - Pro tier quotas section added
  - Updated AmazonQFullAccess with additional permissions
- **November 13**:
  - Updated AmazonQDeveloperAccess managed policy
  - Plugins feature - chat about metrics from third-party tools
  - User activity reports enabled
- **November 8**: Customizations section updated with file/directory naming limitations
- **November 5**: Clarified Amazon Q Developer Pro subscription instructions
- **November 1**: Inline chat feature - transform code with new inline chat

#### October 2024
- **October 29**: Updated managed policies (AmazonQFullAccess, AmazonQDeveloperAccess)
- **October 28**:
  - Customization correction - codebase must be in folder, not bucket root
  - Prompt logging section clarified
- **October 24**: Amazon S3 bucket policy JSON syntax error fixed
- **October 22**: Expanded features chapter significantly
- **October 3**:
  - Console-to-Code now generally available (integrates with EC2, VPC, RDS)
  - New policy for using Amazon Q CLI with AWS CloudShell

#### September 2024
- **September 16**: Significantly simplified and restructured Getting Started chapter

#### August 2024
- **August 15**: CodeWhisperer endpoint needed for IDE VPC access - both `q` and `codewhisperer` endpoints required

#### July 2024
- **July 18**: New endpoint using string `q` instead of `codewhisperer`
- **July 12**: Customizations feature now generally available
- **July 10**:
  - Chatting about customizations (Preview)
  - Updated AmazonQFullAccess managed policy
- **July 9**:
  - New AmazonQDeveloperAccess managed policy
  - Updated Amazon Q Developer admin policy with `sso:ListProfiles`

#### June 2024
- **June 19**:
  - New trusted access section
  - Improved Getting Started chapter with account instances support
- **June 6**:
  - Updated code examples with C and C++
  - Improved C# examples
  - Updated AmazonQFullAccess with additional permissions

#### April 2024
- **April 30**:
  - New service-linked role: AWSServiceRoleForUserSubscriptions
  - New service-linked role: AWSServiceRoleForAmazonQDeveloper
  - New managed policy: AWSServiceRoleForUserSubscriptionPolicy
  - New managed policy: AWSServiceRoleForAmazonQDeveloperPolicy
  - General availability release
  - Amazon CodeWhisperer merged into Amazon Q Developer
  - Service and guide renamed to "Amazon Q Developer"

#### March 2024
- **March 29**: New permission required - ListConversations action for console chat
- **March 5**: New data protection topic - content used for service improvement

#### January 2024
- **January 25**: Instructions for adding Amazon Q to Slack and Microsoft Teams

#### November 2023
- **November 28**: Initial preview release

---

## Feature Summary

### Core Features
- **Chat Interface**: IDE chat, console chat, command-line chat, chat in applications (Slack, Teams)
- **Code Transformation**: Java, .NET, mainframe, VMware transformation with multi-diff support
- **Code Generation**: Feature development, inline suggestions, documentation generation
- **Code Review**: Automated and manual code reviews with issue detection and threading
- **Test Generation**: Unit test generation with `/test` command (deprecated for GitLab)
- **Security Analysis**: Code scanning and security issue detection

### Advanced Capabilities
- **Customizations**: Support for custom knowledge bases and project-specific context
- **Memory Banks**: Context management for chat sessions
- **Project Rules**: Standards and best practices for GitLab and GitHub
- **MCP Integration**: Model Context Protocol servers (local and remote with OAuth)
- **Custom Agents**: CLI agent customization with specific tools and permissions
- **Plugins**: Third-party tool integration (CloudZero, etc.)
- **Context Management**:
  - Code as context (classes, functions, variables)
  - Image context support
  - Context pinning
  - Context hooks (deprecated)

### Deployment & Access
- **Subscription Models**: Free tier, Pro tier, Builder ID upgrades
- **Authentication**: IAM Identity Center, Builder ID, AWS SSO
- **Supported IDEs**:
  - Visual Studio Code
  - JetBrains (IntelliJ, PyCharm, etc.)
  - Visual Studio
  - Eclipse (preview)
- **Platforms**:
  - Windows, macOS, Linux
  - AWS Console
  - GitHub, GitLab
  - Chat applications
- **Regions**: Multiple AWS Regions with cross-region inference support, Frankfurt support added

### Security & Compliance
- **Data Encryption**: Customer managed keys (CMK) support
- **VPC Endpoints**: Interface endpoints for private access
- **Proxy Configuration**: Corporate proxy support (not PAC files)
- **IAM Policies**:
  - AmazonQFullAccess
  - AmazonQDeveloperAccess
  - Customization policies
  - Service-linked role policies
- **Telemetry**: User activity reports, prompt logging, opt-out options

### Administration
- **User Management**: User subscriptions across multiple AWS accounts
- **Profile Sharing**: Cross-account profile configuration
- **Dashboard**: Usage metrics and user activity
- **User Activity Reports**: Generated at midnight UTC, 1,000 users per file
- **Troubleshooting**: Comprehensive logs and diagnostics

### CLI Tools
- **Amazon Q CLI**: Chat, context management, prompt management, agent creation
- **Kiro CLI**: Renamed from Q CLI
- **QCT CLI**: Command-line code transformation tool with job history

---

## API & Technical Details

### Permissions & Actions
- `ListConversations` - Required for console chat
- `kms:Decrypt`, `kms:DescribeKey`, `kms:Encrypt`, `kms:GenerateDataKey` - KMS operations
- `sso:CreateInstance`, `sso:ListProfiles` - IAM Identity Center operations

### Endpoints
- Standard Q endpoint: `q.<region>.amazonaws.com`
- Cross-region: `https://q.eu-central-1.amazonaws.com/`
- CodeWhisperer endpoint still required for some VPC configurations

### Quotas & Limits
- **Pro Tier Quotas**: Documented in dedicated quotas section
- **Account Limits**:
  - Maximum 20 AWS accounts per Region per organization (updated Feb 2026, previously 10)
  - Maximum 50 AWS accounts per organization for enabling Amazon Q Developer

### Language Support
As of November 2024: Java, Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, C, C++, C#, Go, Rust, Ruby, PHP, Kotlin, Scala, Dart, Lua, R, Swift, SystemVerilog, PowerShell, JSON, YAML, and more

---

## Deprecations & Removals
1. **Code Transformation**: Deprecated for GitHub and GitLab
2. **IDE Agents**: `/dev`, `/doc`, `/test`, `/review` agents replaced by agentic chat
3. **GitLab Quick Actions**: `/q dev (revise)` and `/q test` removed
4. **Context Hooks**: Deprecated in favor of agent hooks
5. **Transformation Website**: Removed (May 2025)
6. **S3 Bucket Allowlisting**: No longer needed for VS Code v1.72.0+
7. **PAC Files**: Not supported for proxy configuration

---

This document history spans from preview release (November 28, 2023) through February 18, 2026, showing continuous feature development and enhancement across all Amazon Q Developer capabilities.
